acceptance_criteria.text: The left sidebar header no longer shows the stacked "Projects", "Workspace", or runtime-mode labels. When an active project exists, that header area shows the active project's name as the primary label; when no project exists, a neutral empty-state header is acceptable.

proof.summary: Sidebar header now renders the active project name or a neutral empty-state label.
Header area keeps only the compact project action button instead of the old stacked labels.

acceptance_criteria.text: Project creation moves out of the always-visible form into a compact `[+ New Project]` button that opens a creation modal. Submitting that modal still creates the project through the existing `window.bigIDE.projects.create` flow.

proof.summary: Adds the compact `[+ New Project]` launcher.
Project modal keeps `project-name-input` and submit flow wired to the existing create action.
Playwright covers the new project modal flow.

acceptance_criteria.text: Session creation moves out of the global sidebar form into a control inside each project item. Creating a session from a project item creates the session for that specific project and keeps the existing session lifecycle actions functional.

proof.summary: Each project card renders its own session creation button.
The session modal submits against the selected project id.
Playwright covers session creation and start/stop from the updated UI.

acceptance_criteria.text: A fresh app state with no persisted projects shows an empty project list with no auto-seeded/demo/placeholder projects. The refactor must not introduce startup seeding in either Electron or web mode.

proof.summary: Web backend persisted state still initializes with an empty `projects` array.
Electron persisted state still initializes with an empty `projects` array.
UI state no longer seeds a default project name.

acceptance_criteria.text: The literal `Web runtime ready` is removed from the UI. Startup and status messaging may remain, but must use neutral wording that works in both runtime targets.

proof.summary: Bootstrap status now uses the neutral `Workspace ready` copy.

proof.command: rg -n "Web runtime ready" src server electron e2e .agent/tasks/refactor
proof.summary: Only `spec.md` still mentions the removed string; app sources do not.

acceptance_criteria.text: The main workspace content removes the extra `main > section` padding, and overlapping/redundant borders or dividers are cleaned up so adjacent shells do not visually double-stack separators.

proof.summary: The main workspace section no longer carries the old padded wrapper.
Session panel area uses a single shell boundary instead of the previous stacked borders.
The panel rail now scrolls inside the cleaned-up workspace container.

acceptance_criteria.text: In the active session header, session chips/badges are presented in a more compact form beneath the session title instead of sharing the same crowded title row, and the Start/Stop controls use a visibly more compact treatment while preserving their current actions.

proof.summary: Session title is separated from the status chips.
Compact badges render beneath the title row.
Start button now uses the small compact treatment.
Stop button now uses the small compact treatment.
Lifecycle controls still work from the compact header.

acceptance_criteria.text: The existing panel toggle/navigation strip (`Panels`, `Agent pinned`, `Files`, `Editor`, etc.) is removed. In its place, the session workspace provides a `[+ New Panel]` entry point that opens a modal for creating additional panels.

proof.summary: Session header exposes `[+ New Panel]` instead of the old toggle strip.
Panel creation options are now rendered in a modal entry flow.
No old panel-strip hooks remain in production source; the only `editor-tabs` match is a negative Playwright assertion.

acceptance_criteria.text: The panel creation flow supports multiple panels of the same type in one session layout, and any panel instance - including an agent panel - can be closed individually.

proof.summary: Panel state is now stored as arrays of unique panel instances per session.
Any individual panel instance can be removed, including Agent.
Playwright closes/reopens Agent and confirms duplicate Files panels can coexist.

acceptance_criteria.text: Session panels can be reordered by drag and drop within the horizontal panel rail, and the rail remains horizontally scrollable so off-screen panels are still reachable.

proof.summary: The panel rail remains horizontally scrollable.
Panel wrappers are draggable.
Drop handling reorders panel instances inside the session rail state.

acceptance_criteria.text: Editor panels no longer use tabs. One editor panel represents one file.

proof.summary: Each editor panel is bound to a single `panel.filePath`.
Playwright asserts `editor-tabs` are absent after opening files.

acceptance_criteria.text: Opening a file from the Files panel creates a new editor panel for that file unless that same file is already open in an existing editor panel; in that case, the app focuses the existing panel instead of creating a duplicate for the same file.

proof.summary: Open-file flow checks for an existing editor panel by matching `filePath`.
A new editor panel is appended only when the file is not already open.
Playwright confirms re-opening the same file keeps one editor panel and a different file opens a second editor panel.

acceptance_criteria.text: The Files panel supports collapsing directories correctly, and directories are collapsed by default when the tree first loads.

proof.summary: Directory expansion state starts empty instead of default-open.
Directory toggle flips from a collapsed default state.
Playwright verifies nested files are hidden until parent directories are expanded.
